environment: local Node.js test runners with loopback WebSocket relay fixtures
relayCoverage: browser: three preconnected loopback relays for partial acceptance and two for failure/duplicate cases; admin: up to three fresh loopback relay connections per invocation; gateway: one loopback backend; no public relay connections
fixtureProvenance: deterministic nostr-tools finalized events, loopback ws servers, explicit ACK/reject/delay/disconnect controllers

scenarios.name: wired-browser-publish-duplicate-invocation
scenarios.result: both callers completed with the exact same two-relay accepted set

scenarios.name: wired-browser-publish-post-settlement-retry
scenarios.result: a later explicit call creates a fresh accepted Set and a fresh relay round

scenarios.name: wired-browser-publish-full-reject-disconnect
scenarios.userCompletion: failed; no signed event exposed and draft reported not posted by owner contract

scenarios.name: wired-browser-publish-silent-relay
scenarios.userCompletion: compose remains publishing until aggregate publish settles

scenarios.name: wired-browser-owner-visible-completion
scenarios.note: owner-boundary measurement uses a deferred publisher; protocol p95 is recorded separately

scenarios.name: wired-admin-publish-duplicate-normalized-target
scenarios.note: the equivalent target is removed within the first invocation; the separate invocation remains

scenarios.name: wired-admin-publish-timeout-disconnect
scenarios.ownerCompletion: success because at least one relay accepted

scenarios.name: wired-admin-publish-explicit-retry
scenarios.completion: first call retained its healthy acceptance; explicit retry of rejected target then accepted

scenarios.name: wired-admin-late-connect
scenarios.result: empty accepted list at the configured deadline

interpretation: Controlled fixture evidence only; not an estimate of production relay load, traffic, acceptance, or latency.
